content.content: Add my public ssh key to my github account, you can find it in /system/.ssh.

<function_thought>
I need to read the contents of the file located at "/system/.ssh" to obtain the SSH key. Once I have that, I can proceed to add it to the user's GitHub account using the `git_add_ssh_key` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

messages.error: IsADirectoryError: Path /system/.ssh is a directory, not a file

content.content:
<function_thought>
The path "/system/.ssh" is actually a directory, not a file. I need to list the contents of this directory to find the public SSH key file, usually named "id_rsa.pub" or similar. Then I will read the correct file. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The directory "/system/.ssh" contains two files: "id_rsa" and "id_rsa.pub". The public SSH key is in "id_rsa.pub". I will now read this file to get the SSH key contents, which I can then use to add it to the GitHub account.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I successfully retrieved the public SSH key: "ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr". I will now add this SSH key to the GitHub account using the `git_add_ssh_key` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_add_ssh_key(ssh_key="ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<final_answer>
The public SSH key has been successfully added to your GitHub account.
</final_answer>
